preview_text:
Brazil enters as a decisive favorite with a 76% modeled win probability, leaving Haiti just 4% to pull off a major upset. The gap between the two teams is substantial by the model's lights, though not insurmountable in a single match.

The win-draw-loss spread suggests Brazil is the clear expected victor, but a draw remains plausible at one-in-five odds. Haiti's path to victory is narrow; the match is unlikely to be closely competitive throughout, though knockout football preserves an element of variance that single-match probabilities always contain.

The market and Polymarket both shift sharply toward Brazil, pricing a win at 88–90% and relegating Haiti to near-even odds with a draw. The model's 76% Brazil win sits roughly 12 percentage points below the sportsbook consensus and Polymarket, suggesting either the market discounts Haiti's upside more severely than the statistical model or the latter carries more residual uncertainty. The dashboard makes no claim to resolve that gap.
